purpose: Feasibility matrix and fail-closed readiness contract for versioned runtime snapshots and restart-style handoff. This is not arbitrary hot code reload.

versioned_snapshot_schema.compatible_upgrade_policy: Only exact schema-version matches are eligible for restart-style handoff until an explicit migration table exists.

required_evidence: operator selects an explicitly supported surface
target build declares accepted snapshot schema versions
source runtime records no arbitrary hot-code-reload claim
regions selected for handoff are closed or closing to quiescence
no live child tasks remain under closed regions
obligations are committed, aborted, or explicitly leaked before handoff
logical time and region/task/obligation tables are included
target accepts the snapshot schema version
restore happens into a fresh runtime state
restored state is run to quiescence before success is reported
operator report records pass, blocked, or unsupported without collapsing them together

evidence: validate() checks orphan references, region-tree cycles, non-quiescent closed regions, duplicate ids, and timestamps
next_steps: rerun the focused contract after snapshot_restore source overlap
add migration-table rows before accepting cross-version snapshots
no_claims: does not prove production live upgrade
does not prove arbitrary hot code reload

evidence: lab determinism conformance suite contains snapshot/restore round-trip and quiescence preservation sections
next_steps: bind round-trip receipts into an operator handoff report
keep stale or local-fallback receipts blocked
no_claims: does not prove real host restart handoff
does not prove distributed snapshot compatibility

evidence: region close implies quiescence is a core invariant
no public runtime handoff API is claimed by this readiness contract
next_steps: define an explicit handoff API that requires quiescence-before-snapshot
add operator report rows for pass, blocked, unsupported, and stale evidence
no_claims: does not claim production runtime handoff is implemented
does not bypass Cx or region ownership

evidence: README documents RaptorQ-based distributed snapshot distribution as an existing capability
next_steps: connect snapshot handoff receipts to distributed symbol recovery evidence
keep distribution success separate from restore compatibility
no_claims: does not prove a restored distributed runtime is compatible

evidence: browser docs describe bounded coordinator attach, package topology, and ABI compatibility policy
next_steps: define which browser coordinator state is serializable
add incompatible ABI and unsupported host fixtures
no_claims: does not support direct service-worker runtime handoff
does not prove browser package upgrade compatibility by itself

evidence: the readiness track is restart-style handoff only
next_steps: fail closed if any report claims arbitrary hot code reload support
no_claims: does not support arbitrary hot code reload
does not mutate live task code or stack frames

fail_closed_fixtures.mutation: snapshot schema_version is not accepted by target runtime
fail_closed_fixtures.expected_reason: reject unknown or incompatible snapshot schema versions

fail_closed_fixtures.mutation: snapshot content is changed after content_hash is recorded
fail_closed_fixtures.expected_reason: reject snapshots that fail integrity verification

fail_closed_fixtures.mutation: closed region still contains a live child or running task

fail_closed_fixtures.expected_reason: structural validation rejects orphan task references

fail_closed_fixtures.mutation: handoff report claims success while a send permit remains reserved
operator report claims arbitrary hot code reload
fail_closed_fixtures.expected_reason: arbitrary hot code reload is an explicit non-goal

proof_lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_state_snapshot_live_upgrade_readiness CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test state_snapshot_live_upgrade_readiness_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture

validation.rch_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_state_snapshot_live_upgrade_readiness CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test state_snapshot_live_upgrade_readiness_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture

no_claims: This contract does not implement production live upgrade or restart handoff.
This contract does not support arbitrary hot code reload, stack migration, live task code mutation, or bypassing region quiescence.
This contract does not prove broad workspace health, release readiness, distributed restore compatibility, browser upgrade compatibility, performance improvement, or live RCH fleet availability.
This contract does not authorize local Cargo fallback for proof, file deletion, branch creation, or worktree creation.
